nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Developer reference for all scripts in the `scripts/` directory, documenting the registry-generator scripts (PowerShell and shell variants), their CLI flags, and the `smoke-generate-endpoint.ps1` smoke test for the `/generate` endpoint.

nodes.name: build_examples.ps1
nodes.summary: Builds Docker images for all three example MCP servers (dice-roller, weather-api, file-utils) and optionally the FusionAL gateway image in a single PowerShell script.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses PowerShell `docker build` commands directly; no error handling — exits on first Docker failure via implicit PowerShell behavior.

nodes.name: generate_registry_from_docker.ps1
nodes.summary: Enumerates running Docker containers (optionally filtered by label or image-name regex) and writes a fresh `core/mcp_registry.json`, backing up the previous file and preserving any `test-server` entry from the backup.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `ConvertFrom-Json` / `ConvertTo-Json` for registry serialization; preserves `test-server` from backup via selective key copy; `Set-StrictMode -Version Latest` enforces strict variable use.

nodes.name: generate_registry_from_docker.sh
nodes.summary: Bash equivalent of `generate_registry_from_docker.ps1`: enumerates running Docker containers with optional label or image-regex filtering, then delegates JSON construction to an inline Python heredoc to write `core/mcp_registry.json`.
nodes.languageNotes: Delegates JSON serialization to an embedded Python heredoc for reliability; uses `set -euo pipefail` for strict error handling; `grep -Ei` applies case-insensitive regex filtering when `--image-regex` is supplied.

nodes.name: smoke-generate-endpoint.ps1
nodes.summary: End-to-end smoke test for the FusionAL `/generate` REST endpoint: posts a generation prompt, extracts the returned port, and polls the generated server's `/health` endpoint until it responds or a timeout is reached.
nodes.languageNotes: Falls back through `API_KEY` then `FUSIONAL_API_KEY` env vars for API key resolution; uses a polling loop with 500ms sleep increments up to `HealthTimeoutSeconds`; outputs a structured JSON result on success.
